commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: send: I will not suffer you to be tempted above that which ye are able to endure; otherwise: "Beware," He says, "of false prophets," for I send unto you no new doctrine; but keep that which ye have received unto the end. <a

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: known: So, again, they who work iniquity know not God, although they preach Him. In this way also, God Who knoweth all men, knoweth not those who work iniquity.

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: you: As he commends repentance to the ungodly, and threatens them with punishment, so he exhorts the godly to patience by holding forth eternal rewards.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Else, both the devil's ingenuity in malice, and God the Lord's in the Discipline by which He fortifies us against the devil's depths,

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: That is, I have not given you laws, observances, and duties, which is another burden.
